The payout is determined by something called the revenue share model.
Here's how it actually works. Every month,
Spotify pools all of its subscription revenue from every country.
They take their cut, roughly 30%, off the top.
Then they divide what's left by the total number of streams across
the entire platform, weighted by where those streams came

US subscriber pays more. So your payout per

It's 5 tenths of a cent if they're in Norway. The same song,
the same stream, the same artist, the same 3
minutes and 47 seconds of music worth different amounts of money depending

That's why the per-stream number you see quoted is an average.

Because the ambiguity benefits them. If nobody knows exactly
